findings.evidence: Mathlib has `def selfAdjointPart : A →ₗ[R] selfAdjoint A` with value `(⅟2 : R) • (x + star x)` and `def skewAdjointPart : A →ₗ[R] skewAdjoint A` with value `(⅟2 : R) • (x - star x)` in `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Star/Module.lean:132` and `:145`; square matrices satisfy `star := conjTranspose` in `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/ConjTranspose.lean:407`, so over `ℝ` this is exactly `(A + Aᵀ)/2` and `(A - Aᵀ)/2`.
findings.fix: Remove these definitions and use `(selfAdjointPart ℝ A : Matrix n n ℝ)` and `(skewAdjointPart ℝ A : Matrix n n ℝ)`, or make only thin compatibility aliases if TauCeti-specific names are required.
findings.issue: `symmetricPart` and `skewPart` reimplement the existing `selfAdjointPart`/`skewAdjointPart` decomposition for `Matrix n n ℝ`.

summary: The new symmetric/skew matrix decomposition duplicates Mathlib's existing star-module self/skew-adjoint decomposition API, which applies directly to real square matrices because matrix star is conjugate transpose and real star is trivial.
